JavaScript Core Concepts (34): Objects — The Quiz Round

We've covered a lot of object stuff, so now it's time for the quiz. Ten questions covering array-of-objects access, reference vs value, JSON formatting, functions as objects, and shallow vs deep copy. Read the code, pick your answer, then check whether you got it. Good way to find out if the object chapter actually stuck.
JavaScript Objects: Call by Reference or Call by Sharing? (Core Concepts 29)

Is JavaScript Call by Reference or Call by Sharing? In this post we walk through evaluation strategies — Call by value, Call by reference, and Call by sharing — with simple examples, and figure out which one JavaScript actually uses when you pass objects around.
JavaScript Core Concepts (ES6 Edition): Notes From Will Huang's Kaohsiung Class

A few of the more fun things I picked up from Will Huang's "JavaScript in Practice: Core Concepts (ES6 Edition)" class in Kaohsiung. Empty arrays that fill themselves with blanks, var that won't get overwritten, why Number is pure evil, and a handful of other JS quirks worth knowing.
